title: gossypium hirsutum var. mexicanum
intro: Gossypium hirsutum var. mexicanum, also known as Mexican cotton, is a variety of the cotton plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial shrub with medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Mexican cotton include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against cell damage. Mexican cotton can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
